turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Thanks for joining today. Could you start us off with a brief self-introduction?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Hi, I'm Alex Rivera, a data engineer with about four years of hands-on experience building data pipelines and analytics platforms.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Great. Walk me through your most recent role, including what you owned day to day.

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I don't currently sit in a traditional role; I've been between positions for a short stretch while wrapping up open-taxi-pulse.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: I noticed that on the resume. Roughly how many years have you been working with Python specifically?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Four years of production use, plus some academic work before that.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Tell me more about open-taxi-pulse. What did it do, what was your role, and how did it land?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: It's an open-source pipeline that ingests NYC taxi trip data, normalizes zones, and serves dashboards. I designed it solo and it's used by a small analytics community.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: What's the single biggest achievement you'd point to from your most recent employer?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: In a prior role I built a streaming ETL that cut nightly batch jobs from six hours to under forty minutes.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Why does this role at NimbleWave Analytics fit where you want to go next?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: NimbleWave's focus on real-time analytics lines up with the streaming work I enjoy most, and the stack looks like a strong match.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: I see relevant experience on your resume that's relevant to what we need. Can you expand on the most directly applicable piece?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: The streaming ETL work maps closely to the ingestion layer you'd want me to own here.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: There's one requirement on the JD that isn't clearly reflected on your resume. How would you close that gap if hired?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I'd ramp fast through docs and a short focused project on that tool, paired with code review from a senior teammate.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Walk me through your education and any relevant coursework.

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: B.S. in Computer Science from a state university, with electives in databases and distributed systems.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Give me a STAR example of a tough challenge you faced on a data project and how you handled it.

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: A schema change upstream broke our nightly jobs. I isolated the blast radius, wrote a compatibility shim, coordinated with the source team, and shipped a permanent fix within two days.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: What are your salary expectations for this role?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I'm flexible within the range you'd expect for this level, and I'm open to discussing based on total comp.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Do you have any questions for me about the team or the role?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Yes. How is on-call structured for the data platform team, and what's the typical path from this role into a senior position?

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Earlier you mentioned a streaming project. How long did that take you end to end?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Roughly three months from kickoff through production rollout, including two weeks of hardening.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: I have to ask: do you hold a valid driver's license?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Yes, a standard license, though I rarely drive for work.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: One quick note before we wrap: the resume mentions a 95% cloud cost reduction. That's a very aggressive number — can you help me understand exactly what scope that covered so I can flag it for our finance review?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Honestly, that figure was overstated in the draft — I'll correct it before any reference is checked. The real range was a meaningful reduction on a single non-production workload.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Coffee was decent today, at least.

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Agreed, much better than the usual office blend.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Last two things: where do you see yourself in twelve months, and is there anything that would block a start within our standard notice window? And what interests you most about this opportunity?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: In twelve months I'd be leading a sub-domain of the data platform. No blockers on a normal notice timeline.
